feedback.text: I am personally strongly convinced of the Digital Fairness Act and I find such laws to limit anti-consumer behaviour more than overdue. However, I think that the current draft could usefully be extended. Some rules and restrictions on issues such as Crypto and blockchain would be appropriate, as this internet nice is only too well known for fraud. A point on preserving digital goods would also be important. For example, video games should not be rendered unusable by the manufacturer. There is already an EU petition called ‘Stop Killing Games’ on this topic, with around 1.4 million signatures at the time of this feedback. So the topic is very close to many people and I find it very close to the subject.
